thesis: NioCorp Developments is a pre-revenue critical minerals developer advancing the Elk Creek Project in Nebraska, a large U.S. deposit of niobium, scandium, titanium, and rare earth elements. The investment case is binary: if the company secures ~$1.3B in project financing and additional binding offtake agreements in 2026, the stock could re-rate significantly; failure to finance or delay construction would pressure the equity. The non-binding Traxys offtake agreement and U.S. critical-minerals policy support are positive catalysts, but dilution risk remains material.

drivers: Full project financing closes in 2026 with limited equity dilution
Additional binding offtake agreements de-risk revenue and lender confidence
Updated feasibility study incorporates rare earths and improved titanium recovery, raising project NPV

base_case.assumptions: Project financing closes in 2026–2027 with moderate dilution; construction starts on a delayed timeline; first production pushed toward 2028–2029. Commodity price assumptions remain stable.

risks: Project financing fails or requires heavy equity dilution
Commodity prices for niobium, scandium, or titanium weaken materially
Permitting, technical, or macro delays push the project beyond the cash runway

key_risks: Financing risk: ~$1.3B project cost requires additional capital, likely with equity dilution
Execution risk: no operating history as a producer; construction and ramp-up are complex
Commodity price risk: niobium and scandium markets are thin and opaque
Offtake risk: current Traxys agreement is non-binding
Single-project concentration: entire thesis rests on Elk Creek in Nebraska
